118520.explanation: Each party in the new council should put forward their candidate alderman so that the gender ratio is coincidental and not predetermined.

118521.explanation: For truly important matters like whether or not to merge with another municipality, a referendum would be desirable, but with a predetermined turnout threshold of, for example, 60%-80%.

118525.explanation: The municipality should stimulate sound initiatives and therefore also provide financial support at the start.

118526.explanation: On the one hand, we are legally obligated under the distribution law, and on the other hand, we have a moral duty to shelter asylum seekers-indeed in a humane way with adequate housing and good agreements with neighbors. An agreement must be kept.

118530.explanation: If it supports the fireworks ban effective in 2026, municipalities could, like other European countries, organize an annual fireworks show together with, for example, the (hospitality) business sector.

118531.explanation: Bulky waste can be brought to the recycling center and paid for with the environmental pass. For certain target groups, pick-up options should be available, but settlement must still be via the environmental pass.

118532.explanation: The current standards are sufficient and do not need to be stricter.

118536.explanation: The local broadcaster is already adequately supported by the municipality; therefore, no EXTRA funds are needed.

118538.explanation: Just as physical education is promoted, music education should also be encouraged. The municipality receives funds from the national government that should be directed to primary schools.

118541.explanation: It is important that we have enough neighborhood police officers according to the applicable standard. Additionally, a small team of enforcement officers (BOAs) is necessary to help maintain street safety. The current number is sufficient, especially if they are better directed.

118542.explanation: Camera surveillance is not only preventative but also helpful in tracking down thieves and vandals. GB supports more camera surveillance in the centers and other necessary locations, as well as in business parks.

118550.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes for families having difficulties (on welfare and debt restructuring) is fortunately already part of our limited poverty policy.

118551.explanation: Currently at 110 percent of the minimum and, as far as GB is concerned, should be increased to 120 percent.

118553.explanation: Given the developments regarding the increasing use of electric and hybrid cars, this seems a necessary development, but with the caveat that these parking spaces should be hybrid, not exclusively for electric vehicles.

118554.explanation: Large parts of our municipality are suitable for a 30 km/h zone, and some already have it. It seems unnecessary to make the entire built-up area a 30 km/h zone. However, more enforcement against speeding on main roads like Marktstraat and Gasthuisstraat would be allowed.

118555.explanation: There is a shortage in care, and organizing care by providers is already a difficult task. Where customization based on religion, culture, or belief is possible, that is fine, but care is for everyone regardless of religion, culture, or belief. (no pillarization)

118556.explanation: Price is an important factor in youth care because costs are skyrocketing, but expertise and proximity are also important, so tendering should not focus solely on price.

118557.explanation: Municipal Interests (Gemeentebelangen) has been and remains against exorbitant increases in property tax (OZB) that shift potential financial problems of the local government to residents. Lowering the OZB again is difficult, but GB stands for raising the OZB only according to the price index.

118558.explanation: Dog owners collectively must bear the costs of facilities provided for dog walking and nuisance. The dog tax must at least cover costs.

118559.explanation: GB supports fair and logical differentiation in tourist tax. This should be in consultation with the sectors, with the principle that 'agreement is agreement,' allowing businesses to plan for tariffs over multiple years. Current rates seem reasonable compared to (hotel) room prices.

118562.explanation: It is not the municipality that determines whether refugees with residence permits get priority over local housing seekers; that is decided by the housing association, following national guidelines.

118564.explanation: If it can contribute to the energy shortage on the power grid in our region and enable agricultural entrepreneurs to build a profitable business, we are not opposed to it.

118566.explanation: We do not have a municipal swimming pool ourselves, making the (transport) costs and time involved in organizing school swimming unreasonable.

118567.explanation: There is already a problem with basic skills, so GB thinks it wise to devote as much time as possible to these skills such as Dutch and arithmetic.

118568.explanation: GB favors a strict drug policy, and a coffeeshop, with all its undesirable side effects and phenomena, does not fit in.

118570.explanation: No high-rise flats are allowed in our municipality other than senior residences with 2 to 3 floors and apartments with a commercial plinth (shops or social-medical).

118571.explanation: Permitting room rentals structurally as a contribution to housing shortage leads to abuses not fitting a village community like Loon op Zand. We are not a student city.

118575.explanation: In the established housing vision, as a municipality and council, we have indicated the housing needs now and in the future, including a substantial number of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes.

118577.explanation: As long as we as a municipality can provide good service levels to citizens at acceptable costs and can properly fulfill our legal tasks, we continue to strive for small-scale independence. At this moment, GB sees no reason to think otherwise.

118520.explanation: The VVD considers it important that a council is formed based on quality, experience, and suitability. For the VVD, quality takes precedence over quotas. However, the VVD does find it important that there is a healthy balance between men and women in the new council.

118521.explanation: Our residents go to the polls during elections to choose their representatives. The VVD values participation highly and likes to engage residents in the process. Residents can participate if they themselves wish to do so.

118525.explanation: We think it is good if residents want to generate sustainable energy together, but we do not see financial support for this as a municipal task.

118526.explanation: In the event of national abolition of the distribution law, we do not believe the municipality should organize an asylum seekers' center. The VVD believes the municipality must comply with legal regulations. As long as the distribution law is in effect, we will act accordingly, where possible through regional cooperation.

118530.explanation: A fireworks show is very costly and we do not see it as a task of the municipality of Loon op Zand.

118531.explanation: Unfortunately, the collection of bulky waste is not free. To keep our municipal costs as low as possible, we offer a facility at the recycling center where you can bring bulky waste.

118532.explanation: The current noise standards are sufficient for the VVD.

118536.explanation: We consider it important that local broadcasters can continue to exist. The VVD does not see a need for extra funding.

118538.explanation: With the SamenLoont card, it is also possible for families with the very lowest incomes to receive music lessons. Primarily, the responsibility for membership lies with the parents of a child.

118541.explanation: We support increasing the number of community enforcement officers (boa's) if this is necessary for safety.

118542.explanation: Camera surveillance is necessary to improve street safety and to track down troublemakers.

118549.explanation: People who receive benefits from society must give something back to society. Ideally, we want people to work; if that is truly not possible, we believe people should participate in socially meaningful activities.

118550.explanation: The VVD believes that all residents are inherently responsible for paying their taxes.

118551.explanation: We find the current income threshold of 120% appropriate.

118553.explanation: Currently, there are more than enough parking spaces for electric cars in our municipality.

118554.explanation: It must be safe within the built-up area. If it is necessary to lower the speed limit for this reason, that is fine. However, standardizing this everywhere is not necessary.

118555.explanation: The VVD supports freedom of choice.

118556.explanation: The quality of youth care is our top priority. Youth care must remain financially manageable.

118557.explanation: The VVD supports tax reduction for our residents. In return, there should be an efficient government.

118558.explanation: Dog tax is an outdated and unfair levy without a clear counterpart. Abolishing it makes things simpler, fairer, and prevents arbitrariness compared to other pets.

118559.explanation: Where tax reduction is possible, we are in favor.

118562.explanation: We believe the municipality must fulfill its legal duty regarding housing status holders.

118564.explanation: The VVD thinks along in terms of possibilities. The owner has control and initiative.

118566.explanation: We find it important that all children obtain a swimming certificate. This is also possible for families with the very lowest incomes through municipal minimum rules.

118567.explanation: We are proud of our Brabant culture, but enabling this in schools is not a municipal task.

118568.explanation: The VVD does not consider a coffee shop appropriate in our municipality.

118570.explanation: To address the housing crisis, we allow adding an extra building layer.

118571.explanation: A solution to the housing shortage is expanding the housing supply. We support options such as studios and other housing solutions. Loon op Zand does not have a target group for room rentals.

118575.explanation: We find it important that there is a healthy mix of housing types and affordability.

118577.explanation: Loon op Zand must remain an independent municipality if financially and organizationally feasible.

118520.explanation: For Pro3, we will always strive for good representation of gender, background, age, etc. We always look for the right person in the right place.

118521.explanation: A simple yes or no is rarely the right answer to politically complex issues. The municipality must continuously consult with residents, which can be done through much better instruments such as a Citizens' Assembly or the Right to Challenge.

118525.explanation: We must take major steps in the energy transition, regardless of what national politics does (or does not do). The municipality must actively help residents, especially those who do not have the resources.

118526.explanation: Pro3 believes we must organize humane reception. In recent years, we have shown that we can do this well with many volunteers from our community. Additionally, this is also an obligation under the distribution law.

118530.explanation: From next year, fireworks will be banned nationwide. Pro3 has long supported a ban on (loud) fireworks. A fireworks show is a nice alternative and also provides opportunities for people to come together for a festive moment. We will support citizen initiatives in this regard.

118531.explanation: In this way, we help people with limited financial means. Recycling also becomes easier. The municipality must take measures to ensure it does not become messy.

118532.explanation: There are good national standards. The municipality must prevent nuisance and hearing damage, for example, by positioning stages and speakers appropriately.

118536.explanation: We consider local broadcasters an important link in the journalistic system.

118538.explanation: Music and cultural development help children in their development, just like sports. Pro3 supports this.

118541.explanation: Community enforcement officers contribute to a safer society but also provide an accessible way to address, for example, youth groups. Additionally, they can provide relief for the police.

118542.explanation: In recent years, there has been an increase in camera surveillance. In stores, but also at people's homes with devices such as video doorbells. Privacy is increasingly at risk because of this.

118549.explanation: People have the right to social assistance. The reasons why they are on assistance can vary. Imposing an obligation goes too far for us.

118550.explanation: Poverty has only increased in recent years. People in debt must be helped.

118551.explanation: More and more people just above the minimum income level can no longer make ends meet.

118553.explanation: To further encourage the use of electric cars, it is necessary that the facilities for them become even more available.

118554.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h everywhere within the built-up area increases the safety of pedestrians and cyclists.

118555.explanation: Care must meet the needs of people. Personal choice fits into that.

118556.explanation: Quality and reliability come first; after that, price is a consideration.

118557.explanation: To maintain the facilities in the municipality, the municipality needs income. The property tax (OZB) is one of them. Lowering the OZB directly affects the level of facilities.

118558.explanation: The dog tax must be spent on cleaning and maintaining dog walking areas and tackling nuisance, but it should not be a source of profit for the municipality.

118559.explanation: As a tourist municipality, we offer tourists a good range of options that is proportional to the level of the tourist tax.

118562.explanation: At the moment, we follow national rules for this. Refugees with a residence permit, along with several other urgent groups, receive and deserve priority.

118564.explanation: In the energy transition, we must take big steps. Solar panels on agricultural land must also be possible. Additionally, this can be an interesting option for farmers to enable diversification or renewal of their business.

118566.explanation: School swimming can especially be a solution for people with low income. We find it important that every child has equal opportunities, including in skills such as swimming. This does not necessarily have to come at the expense of lesson time, but can also take the form of subsidies.

118567.explanation: Children must learn to read and write well; that is a priority.

118568.explanation: A coffee shop often causes too much nuisance.

118570.explanation: More high-rise buildings are allowed, up to three or four residential floors. Tall residential towers do not fit in our villages.

118571.explanation: With the current housing shortages, this is a solution that can contribute somewhat on a small scale. This makes better use of square meters in homes. However, there must be enforcement against undesirable use, for example by labor migrants.

118575.explanation: Building affordable rental and owner-occupied homes is necessary to meet demand, but mid-priced homes are also needed to ensure flow; a good mix is important.

118577.explanation: It is important that Loon op Zand can continue to meet the tasks the municipality has. In our view, that is best achieved as an independent municipality close to its residents.

118520.explanation: For the CDA, the quality of individuals comes first. Together, they form a collegial board.

118521.explanation: The municipal council consists of elected representatives. Those from the CDA stand among the people. They know what is alive in society and what is needed for our residents.

118526.explanation: The municipality must comply with the national requirement from the Spread Act. A temporary asylum seekers' center with good agreements fits this. A permanent asylum seekers' center is not an option.

118530.explanation: This does not fit with a rural municipality. Moreover, the Efteling already provides sufficient fireworks displays.

118531.explanation: Bulky waste can be disposed of free of charge at the recycling center.

118538.explanation: This is the responsibility of schools, which receive funding for this from the national government. If children from low-income families need financial support for this, this is already possible through existing schemes.

118541.explanation: Our municipality already has enough enforcement officers. Their visibility should be increased to enhance the feeling of safety.

118542.explanation: It is not necessary for the municipality to install more cameras. Private cameras are also used during emergencies and such. Technology assists us in this.

118549.explanation: You get what you give. That is the guiding principle for the CDA.

118550.explanation: People with a low income deserve attention so they do not financially sink through the ice.

118551.explanation: The current threshold at 120 percent is already higher than the national average. That is sufficient to prevent financial hardship.

118553.explanation: That is not necessary now. There are enough public charging points available.

118554.explanation: This applies to Kaatsheuvel. It has already been included in the new mobility policy.

118555.explanation: Receiving care is the most important thing. Faith, culture, or beliefs make no difference in this.

118556.explanation: Price is important, but what you get in return is even more so. Preventive youth care is much more important to ensure young people do not go astray.

118557.explanation: The municipality ranks highest as the most expensive municipality in the region. The gap with surrounding municipalities should be smaller to keep our municipality attractive.

118558.explanation: The dog tax is not a source of income. The amount should be cost-covering.

118559.explanation: The amount should remain proportional to stay attractive for recreational tourists. An annual indexing is therefore logical. If financially possible, a reduction may be considered.

118562.explanation: We must comply with national laws and regulations, but our own residents should not be the victims of this.

118564.explanation: The agreements state that generation on roofs should be looked at first, and only as a last resort on good agricultural land.

118566.explanation: We do not have a municipal swimming pool. Of course, families with a tight budget already receive support for swimming lessons.

118567.explanation: It is certainly necessary to be aware of your own language and the history of the place you come from.

118568.explanation: This does not fit within our rural municipality.

118570.explanation: This does not suit a rural municipality.

118571.explanation: If we can help young people with that, we should make it possible.

118575.explanation: Within the current housing vision, agreements have been made to give starters the possibility of a purchase or rental home.

118577.explanation: As long as the municipality is financially sound enough and our residents want it, we will remain independent.

118520.explanation: If the content of the portfolio and the quality of the candidate match: that is much more important.

118521.explanation: If it concerns a very important issue, such as the municipality's right to exist, then that can certainly be done by means of a referendum.

118525.explanation: There are sufficient existing provisions for that. The municipality has no resources for this.

118526.explanation: This has already been unanimously decided by the council on December 11, 2025. However, the number has been capped at 165 and some preconditions regarding safety for residents have been set.

118530.explanation: The show would preferably be combined with an event. This is to bring people together to usher in the new year.

118532.explanation: At the same time, those must take place in locations at a considerable distance from the built-up area.

118536.explanation: The local broadcaster should be more creative in tapping funding sources.

118538.explanation: Unless music lessons are part of a range of elective subjects from which a child can choose.

118541.explanation: For now, our villages are relatively safe: so no immediate necessity. Other issues like traffic safety are another chapter that needs attention, but that can be done without BOAs (community enforcement officers).

118542.explanation: So far, there has been hardly any reason for this.

118549.explanation: The statement is too general; the question arises why people are in this situation.

118550.explanation: The applicable rules have recently been discussed and found useful.

118551.explanation: If it means financial aid, then we disagree; but if it is help in an immaterial sense (such as the Loon pass), then that should be possible.

118553.explanation: The current supply level seems to be in balance with the demand for public charging points. The balance between electric and fossil propulsion is being closely monitored.

118554.explanation: Two caveats. On through roads in built-up areas, 50 km/h is fine. The second caveat is that setting 30 km/h zones is fairly pointless without regular enforcement.

118555.explanation: Much care provision is already subject to long waiting lists. We should not make the care system more complex by involving religious beliefs.

118556.explanation: Certainly, the price level is not the only determining factor. Quality assurance is important. The relationship with the care package determines the overall quality.

118557.explanation: The current property tax (OZB) cannot be lowered any further, as revenues from this tax have been raised to the maximum by this coalition and municipal expenditures have been adjusted accordingly.

118558.explanation: As such, the dog tax does not significantly contribute to total income. The administrative hassle can better be avoided.

118559.explanation: The reality is that this tax has been set almost at the maximum by the council. There is no need for a further increase. Toekomstig KLM wants to safeguard that.

118562.explanation: As long as our youths and those transitioning still have to wait years, this is not a realistic statement.

118564.explanation: An agricultural business is not meant for that, unless the company ceases activities and the municipality explicitly permits it.

118566.explanation: Every year many children drown due to the lack of school swimming. We must prevent that. Unfortunately, our municipality lacks a basic provision.

118567.explanation: Some attention to one’s own language and cultural habits strengthens one's identity. Schools can support this.

118568.explanation: We no longer smoke, but a cup of coffee is always nice ;)

118570.explanation: We would like to preserve our village structure, but limit the number of floors to a maximum of four.

118571.explanation: That seems a wonderful temporary measure that fits in solving the housing shortage.

118575.explanation: No, absolutely not; Loon op Zand must be able to house all social layers.

118577.explanation: The municipality must make choices. So far, the council has not dared to make choices; at the end of 2027, an evaluation report will be presented. If the municipality proves not to be vital, dialogue about a merger will be necessary.

statements.title: Loon op Zand should remain an independent municipality.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Loon op Zand.

titleParts.text: In Loon op Zand moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Loon op Zand should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.text: In Loon op Zand moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: High-rise residential blocks may be allowed in Loon op Zand.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabantian dialect.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The residents of Loon op Zand should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Loon op Zand moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Loon op Zand should mainly build cheap owner-occupied and rental homes.
